ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / ARIZONA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Arizona Dietitian Registration

Arizona Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Arizona run through No Dedicated State Licensing Board. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • CDR Registration Card - Your active CDR card serves as your primary credential in Arizona.
  • ACEND Verification Statement - Proof of completion of your accredited degree program and supervised practice.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing N/A — No State License Required
  • Fingerprinting Not required for most applicants.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Arizona has no state licensing requirements to transfer. Your national CDR credential (RD/RDN) is recognized directly. No state endorsement process exists.

  1. STEP 1

    Complete Required Education

    Complete a didactic program in dietetics and a supervised practice program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

    Fee: $0 (State Fee) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

  2. STEP 2

    Pass the Registration Examination

    Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

    Fee: $0 (State Fee) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

  3. STEP 3

    Maintain Registration

    Maintain registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) by completing 75 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) every 5 years.

    Fee: $0 (State Fee) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$200
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$0 (State Fee)

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
No Dedicated State Licensing Board
Phone
N/A
Website
cdrnet.org/
Application page
See board website

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Arizona follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours.
Renewal fee
$0 (State Fee)
Continuing education
75 CPE hours every 5 years

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Arizona require a state dietitian license?

No. Arizona does not mandate state-specific licensure for dietitians. Your national CDR registration as a Registered Dietitian (RD) or Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) is the recognized credential.

What does it cost to practice as a dietitian in Arizona?

There is no state application or renewal fee. The CDR national exam costs $200, and the annual CDR maintenance fee is $80 (increasing to $90 in 2027).

Is there pending legislation to require state licensure in Arizona?

As of 2026, Arizona does not have an active, enacted state licensure law for dietitians. Legislative efforts (such as H.B. 2820 from 2021) have been introduced in the past.

Does Arizona have reciprocity for dietitians?

Arizona is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Arizona?

This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?

There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$0 (State Fee)

Processing time

N/A — No State License Required

Fingerprinting

Not required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours. · $0 (State Fee)

Licensing board

No Dedicated State Licensing Board
